comment_text.sn35.93:1.1: Yājñavalkya taught that the ultimate Self was “this great reality, infinite, unbounded, a sheer mass of consciousness” (idaṁ mahadbhūtam anantam apāraṁ vijñānaghana eva, Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 2.4.12). It arises as an individuated self from the mass of particularized elements making up the world, but for the realized being it returns to its essential oneness with Divinity. Thus Yājñavalkya spoke of the “uncognized cognizer”, the eternal and changeless core of awareness at the heart of the ephemeral world of changing illusions. Rejecting this, the Buddha asserts that consciousness, far from being an eternal or mysterious essence, is dependent on the dualistic structure of experience.
comment_text.sn35.93:1.10: Compare the subtly different usage at MN 28:8.2.
comment_text.sn35.93:1.14: The three aggregates of feeling, choices (saṅkhārā, here expressed as ceteti, “intends”), and perception are dependent on contact.

root_text.sn35.93:1.2: Kathañca, bhikkhave, dvayaṁ paṭicca viññāṇaṁ sambhoti?
root_text.sn35.93:1.3: Cakkhuñca paṭicca rūpe ca uppajjati cakkhuviññāṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.93:1.6: Itthetaṁ dvayaṁ calañceva byathañca aniccaṁ vipariṇāmi aññathābhāvi.
root_text.sn35.93:1.8: Yopi hetu yopi paccayo cakkhuviññāṇassa uppādāya, sopi hetu sopi paccayo anicco vipariṇāmī aññathābhāvī.
root_text.sn35.93:1.9: Aniccaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, paccayaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ cakkhuviññāṇaṁ kuto niccaṁ bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.93:1.10: Yā kho, bhikkhave, imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ saṅgati sannipāto samavāyo, ayaṁ vuccati cakkhusamphasso.
root_text.sn35.93:1.12: Yopi hetu yopi paccayo cakkhusamphassassa uppādāya, sopi hetu sopi paccayo anicco vipariṇāmī aññathābhāvī.
root_text.sn35.93:1.13: Aniccaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, paccayaṁ paṭicca uppanno cakkhusamphasso kuto nicco bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.93:1.14: Phuṭṭho, bhikkhave, vedeti, phuṭṭho ceteti, phuṭṭho sañjānāti.
root_text.sn35.93:1.15: Itthetepi dhammā calā ceva byathā ca aniccā vipariṇāmino aññathābhāvino …pe…
root_text.sn35.93:2.1: Jivhañca paṭicca rase ca uppajjati jivhāviññāṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.93:2.4: Itthetaṁ dvayaṁ calañceva byathañca aniccaṁ vipariṇāmi aññathābhāvi.
root_text.sn35.93:2.6: Yopi hetu yopi paccayo jivhāviññāṇassa uppādāya, sopi hetu sopi paccayo anicco vipariṇāmī aññathābhāvī.
root_text.sn35.93:2.7: Aniccaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, paccayaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ jivhāviññāṇaṁ, kuto niccaṁ bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.93:2.8: Yā kho, bhikkhave, imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ saṅgati sannipāto samavāyo, ayaṁ vuccati jivhāsamphasso.
root_text.sn35.93:2.10: Yopi hetu yopi paccayo jivhāsamphassassa uppādāya, sopi hetu sopi paccayo anicco vipariṇāmī aññathābhāvī.
root_text.sn35.93:2.11: Aniccaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, paccayaṁ paṭicca uppanno jivhāsamphasso, kuto nicco bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.93:2.12: Phuṭṭho, bhikkhave, vedeti, phuṭṭho ceteti, phuṭṭho sañjānāti.
root_text.sn35.93:2.13: Itthetepi dhammā calā ceva byathā ca aniccā vipariṇāmino aññathābhāvino …pe…
root_text.sn35.93:3.1: Manañca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṁ.
root_text.sn35.93:3.4: Itthetaṁ dvayaṁ calañceva byathañca aniccaṁ vipariṇāmi aññathābhāvi.
root_text.sn35.93:3.6: Yopi hetu yopi paccayo manoviññāṇassa uppādāya, sopi hetu sopi paccayo anicco vipariṇāmī aññathābhāvī.
root_text.sn35.93:3.7: Aniccaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, paccayaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ manoviññāṇaṁ, kuto niccaṁ bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.93:3.8: Yā kho, bhikkhave, imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ saṅgati sannipāto samavāyo, ayaṁ vuccati manosamphasso.
root_text.sn35.93:3.10: Yopi hetu yopi paccayo manosamphassassa uppādāya, sopi hetu sopi paccayo anicco vipariṇāmī aññathābhāvī.
root_text.sn35.93:3.11: Aniccaṁ kho pana, bhikkhave, paccayaṁ paṭicca uppanno manosamphasso, kuto nicco bhavissati.
root_text.sn35.93:3.12: Phuṭṭho, bhikkhave, vedeti, phuṭṭho ceteti, phuṭṭho sañjānāti.
root_text.sn35.93:3.13: Itthetepi dhammā calā ceva byathā ca aniccā vipariṇāmino aññathābhāvino.
root_text.sn35.93:3.14: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dvayaṁ paṭicca viññāṇaṁ sambhotī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.93:1.1: “Mendicants, consciousness originates dependent on a duality.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.3: Eye consciousness arises dependent on the eye and sights.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.4: The eye is impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.5: Sights are impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.6: So this duality is tottering and toppling; it’s impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.7: Eye consciousness is impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.8: And the causes and reasons that give rise to eye consciousness are also impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.9: But since eye consciousness has arisen dependent on conditions that are impermanent, how could it be permanent?
translation_text.sn35.93:1.10: The meeting, coming together, and joining together of these three things is called eye contact.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.11: Eye contact is also impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.12: And the causes and reasons that give rise to eye contact are also impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.13: But since eye contact has arisen dependent on conditions that are impermanent, how could it be permanent?
translation_text.sn35.93:1.14: Contacted one feels; contacted one intends; contacted one perceives.
translation_text.sn35.93:1.15: So these things too are tottering and toppling; they’re impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:2.1: Ear consciousness … Nose consciousness … Tongue consciousness … Body consciousness …
translation_text.sn35.93:3.1: Mind consciousness arises dependent on the mind and ideas.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.2: The mind is impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.3: Ideas are impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.4: So this duality is tottering and toppling; it’s impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.5: Mind consciousness is impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.6: And the causes and reasons that give rise to mind consciousness are also impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.7: But since mind consciousness has arisen dependent on conditions that are impermanent, how could it be permanent?
translation_text.sn35.93:3.8: The meeting, coming together, and joining together of these three things is called mind contact.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.9: Mind contact is also impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.10: And the causes and reasons that give rise to mind contact are also impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.11: But since mind contact has arisen dependent on conditions that are impermanent, how could it be permanent?
translation_text.sn35.93:3.12: Contacted one feels; contacted one intends; contacted one perceives.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.13: So these things too are tottering and toppling; they’re impermanent, decaying, and perishing.
translation_text.sn35.93:3.14: This is how consciousness originates dependent on a duality.”
